# Accessibility Instructions

Use these instructions for all UI implementation and review tasks.

## Accessibility baseline

- Prefer semantic HTML before ARIA.
- Use ARIA only when native semantics are insufficient.
- Ensure full keyboard operability.
- Ensure visible focus indicators remain intact.
- Provide accessible names for controls and landmarks.

## Interaction guidance

- Support keyboard navigation for dialogs, menus, tabs, and custom widgets.
- Manage focus intentionally for modals and dynamic content.
- Announce asynchronous state changes when users need that feedback.

## Content and visual guidance

- Preserve meaningful heading hierarchy.
- Use labels, helper text, and error text clearly.
- Avoid color-only meaning.
- Maintain contrast and readability.

## Review focus

- semantic structure
- label associations
- keyboard support
- focus management
- screen reader clarity
- contrast and zoom resilience
